chunks.en: When I pick up the office phone, I say, "Hello, this is Sato from the IT team."
chunks.ja: 会社の電話に出るときは、「もしもし、ITチームの佐藤です」と言います。

chunks.en: When I have to transfer a call, I say, "Please hold on, I'll connect you now."
chunks.ja: 電話を取り次ぐときは、「少々お待ちください、今おつなぎします」と言います。

chunks.en: When the person is out, I take a message and write down the caller's name and number.
chunks.ja: 担当者が不在のときは、伝言を受けて、相手の名前と電話番号をメモします。

chunks.en: To be honest, I prefer chat and email because I can check the details later.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、私はチャットやメールの方が好きです。後で内容を確認できるからです。

outline: Opening: how I greet callers on the office phone
Point: transferring calls and putting people on hold
Point: taking a message when someone is out
Wrap-up: why I prefer chat and email over calls

modelTalk.text: Hi. When the office phone rings, I pick it up fast. I say, "Good morning, this is the sales team, Kenji speaking." Then I ask, "How can I help you?" I try to sound friendly and clear. Sometimes the call is for someone else. So I say, "Just a moment, please. I'll put you through." If I need time, I say, "Can I put you on hold?" When the person is out, I take a message. I write down the name, the number, and the reason. Then I read it back to check. I say, "I'll pass this on when he's back." Honestly, I prefer chat and email. I can think before I write. Phone calls make me a bit nervous. But they're faster for urgent things. So I'm slowly getting used to them. Practice really helps me feel calm.

chunks.en: When I pick up the office phone, I say, "Hello, this is Tanaka from the IT team."
chunks.ja: 会社の電話に出るときは、「もしもし、ITチームの田中です」と言います。

chunks.en: When I have to transfer a call, I say, "Please hold on, I'll put you through."
chunks.ja: 電話を取り次ぐときは、「少々お待ちください、おつなぎします」と言います。

chunks.en: When the person is out, I take a message and write down the caller's name and number.
chunks.ja: 担当者が不在のときは、伝言を受けて、相手の名前と電話番号をメモします。

chunks.en: To be honest, I prefer chat and email because I can check the details later.
chunks.ja: 正直に言うと、後で内容を確認できるので、チャットやメールの方が好きです。

outline: Opening: how I greet callers on the office phone
Point 1: transferring calls and putting people on hold
Point 2: taking a message when someone is out
Wrap-up: why I prefer chat or email over calls

modelTalk.text: Okay, so at work, when the phone rings, I pick it up quickly. I say, "Good morning, this is Sales. How can I help you?" I try to sound friendly and calm. Sometimes the caller wants someone else. So I say, "Sure, let me put you through. Please hold for a moment." If the line is busy, I ask them to wait or call back. When the person they want is out, I take a message. I write down the name, the number, and what they need. Then I read it back to check. I always say I'll pass it on. Honestly, I like chat and email more than calls. With a message, I've got time to think. Phone calls make me a bit nervous. But I'm getting better with practice, and that feels good.
